hypothesis: If pre-trained single-gas neural networks are used as feature extractors within a graph neural network architecture that explicitly encodes gas identity as nodes and mixture composition as node attributes, then the resulting model will more accurately and generalizably predict mixture plasma etching rates across unseen process conditions and gas fractions compared to a standard feedforward neural network trained directly on mixture data, because the graph structure captures pairwise gas interactions while leveraging transferable single-gas process knowledge.
